# UC-22.59.15 · IACS UR E26 ship-level cyber-resilience attestation register — class-society notation evidence chain

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*New ships must have a cyber-safety certificate from their classification society. We keep a list of every ship in the fleet and its certificate, and we send a warning if any ship is missing one or about to expire.*

---

## Description

Tracks every E26-in-scope vessel against the class-society attestation register; raises an alert when no attestation exists, an attestation expires, an attestation is anchored to the wrong revision, or expiry is within the renewal window.

## Value

Without an E26 attestation, a newly contracted vessel cannot be entered into class; the company exposes the vessel to flag-State scrutiny, charterer rejection, and insurance loss. PSC inspectors routinely cross-check E26 status on vessels keel-laid post-July-2024.

## Implementation

(1) Build the attestation KV Store. (2) Wire the company technical department's class-society correspondence into the register (DNV CSManager, ABS Eagle.org, LR Class Direct, BV Veristar). (3) Schedule UC-22.59.15 daily; route P2 for breach and P3 for expiring soon.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.15` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `iacs_clause="IACS-UR-E26-r4"`. Every row pins (imo_number, keel_laid, class_society, attestation_id, e26_revision, attestation_at, expires_at, class_notation, status).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A vessel contracted on 1 August 2024 is missing an E26 attestation 7 days after delivery. UC-22.59.15 MUST flag status=breach-no-attestation.

### Negative scenario

Vessel has a current DNV CyberSecure attestation valid through 2029. UC-22.59.15 MUST record status=compliant.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.1 and UC-22.59.3 in production so vessel + asset registers are authoritative.
- Company technical department feeds class-society correspondence into the register.

## Step 1 — Attestation KV Store

```ini
[iacs_ur_e26_attestation_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.imo_number = string
field.attestation_id = string
field.e26_revision = string  # Rev.1 | Rev.2 | Rev.3 | Rev.4
field.class_society = string  # DNV | ABS | LR | BV | ClassNK | CCS | KR | RINA | IRS | RS | PRS | CRS
field.class_notation = string  # CyberSecure | CyberSafety | ShipRight | Cyber-Resilient
field.attestation_at = time
field.expires_at = time
field.surveyor = string
```

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.15: IACS UR E26 Attestation Register]
cron_schedule = 0 5 * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.15,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IACS-UR-E26-r4
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Maritime-specific notes

- **IACS UR E26 evolution**: Rev.4 became effective 1 July 2024; future revisions (Rev.5, Rev.6) will need a fleet-wide upgrade campaign managed via this UC.
- **Class-notation aliases**: each class society has its own brand; the `class_notation` field must use a canonical map maintained in `class_notation_lookup`.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup imo_doc_verification_lookup
| eval e26_in_scope = if(keel_laid >= strptime("2024-07-01", "%Y-%m-%d"), "true", "false")
| where e26_in_scope = "true"
| lookup iacs_ur_e26_attestation_lookup imo_number OUTPUT attestation_id e26_revision attestation_at expires_at class_society class_notation
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_to_expiry = case(isnotnull(expires_at), round((expires_at - now_ts) / 86400, 1), 1=1, null)
| eval status = case(
    isnull(attestation_id), "breach-no-attestation",
    isnotnull(expires_at) AND expires_at < now_ts, "breach-expired",
    days_to_expiry < 90, "warning-expiring",
    e26_revision != "Rev.4" AND keel_laid >= strptime("2024-07-01", "%Y-%m-%d"), "breach-revision-mismatch",
    1=1, "compliant")
| where status NOT IN ("compliant")
| sort 0 days_to_expiry
| table imo_number ship_name keel_laid class_society attestation_id e26_revision attestation_at expires_at days_to_expiry status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Fleet E26 attestation register table. (Panel 2) Expiring-soon timeline (90/30 days). (Panel 3) Class-notation distribution. (Panel 4) Top revisions in production (Rev.3 / Rev.4).

## Known False Positives

1. **Keel-laid date ambiguity**: contract date vs keel-laying date; honour the IACS interpretation that E26 applies to contracts placed on or after 1 July 2024. 2. **Class transfer**: when a vessel transfers between class societies, both old and new attestations may briefly co-exist; the register must keep only the active one.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_15_exceptions` with the Company Security Officer documented approval.

## References

- [IACS UR E26 — Cyber Resilience of Ships, Rev.4 (2024)](https://iacs.org.uk/publications/unified-requirements/)
- [DNV Rules for Classification — CyberSecure (Pt.6 Ch.5 Sec.21)](https://www.dnv.com/maritime/insights/topics/maritime-cyber-security/index.html)
- [ABS Guide for Cybersecurity Implementation for the Marine and Offshore Industries](https://ww2.eagle.org/en/rules-and-resources/rules-and-guides.html)
